   TXLEVEL,
   RXLEVEL)
  with Size => 1;

enumeration_literals.name: TXLEVEL
enumeration_literals.description: All-ones if RX FIFO level < N, otherwise all-zeroes

record_types.name: CTRL_Register

fields.name: SM_ENABLE
fields.description:
After a write operation all bits in the field are cleared (set to
zero). Write 1 to instantly clear internal SM state which may be
otherwise difficult to access and will affect future execution.\n\n
Specifically, the following are cleared: input and output shift
counters; the contents of the input shift register; the delay
counter; the waiting-on-IRQ state; any stalled instruction written to
SMx_INSTR or run by OUT/MOV EXEC; any pin write left asserted due to
OUT_STICKY.

fields.name: SM_RESTART
fields.description:
After a write operation all bits in the field are cleared (set to
zero). Restart a state machine's clock divider from an initial phase
of 0. Clock dividers are free-running, so once started, their output
(including fractional jitter) is completely determined by the
integer/fractional divisor configured in SMx_CLKDIV. This means that,
if multiple clock dividers with the same divisor are restarted
simultaneously, by writing multiple 1 bits to this field, the
execution clocks of those state machines will run in precise
lockstep.\n\n Note that setting/clearing SM_ENABLE does not stop the
clock divider from running, so once multiple state machines' clocks
are synchronised, it is safe to disable/reenable a state machine,
whilst keeping the clock dividers in sync.\n\n Note also that
CLKDIV_RESTART can be written to whilst the state machine is running,
and this is useful to resynchronise clock dividers after the divisors
(SMx_CLKDIV) have been changed on-the-fly.

fields.name: CLKDIV_RESTART
fields.description: unspecified

record_types.name: DBG_CFGINFO_Register

fields.name: FIFO_DEPTH
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_6_7
fields.description:
Read-only. The number of state machines this PIO instance is equipped
with.

fields.name: SM_COUNT
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_12_15
fields.description:
Read-only. The size of the instruction memory, measured in units of
one instruction

fields.name: IMEM_SIZE
fields.description: unspecified

record_types.name: FDEBUG_Register

fields.name: RXSTALL
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_4_7
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. RX FIFO underflow (i.e. read-on-empty by the system)
has occurred. Write 1 to clear. Note that read-on-empty does not
perturb the state of the FIFO in any way, but the data returned by
reading from an empty FIFO is undefined, so this flag generally only
becomes set due to some kind of software error.

fields.name: RXUNDER
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_12_15
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. TX FIFO overflow (i.e. write-on-full by the system) has
occurred. Write 1 to clear. Note that write-on-full does not alter
the state or contents of the FIFO in any way, but the data that the
system attempted to write is dropped, so if this flag is set, your
software has quite likely dropped some data on the floor.

fields.name: TXOVER
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_20_23
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. State machine has stalled on empty TX FIFO during a
blocking PULL, or an OUT with autopull enabled. Write 1 to clear.

fields.name: TXSTALL
fields.description: unspecified

record_types.name: PIO_Peripheral

fields.name: CTRL
fields.description: FIFO status register

fields.name: FSTAT
fields.description: FIFO debug register

fields.name: FDEBUG
fields.description: FIFO levels

fields.name: FLEVEL
fields.description:
Direct write access to the TX FIFO for this state machine. Each write
pushes one word to the FIFO. Attempting to write to a full FIFO has
no effect on the FIFO state or contents, and sets the sticky
FDEBUG_TXOVER error flag for this FIFO.

fields.name: TXF0
fields.description:
Direct write access to the TX FIFO for this state machine. Each write
pushes one word to the FIFO. Attempting to write to a full FIFO has
no effect on the FIFO state or contents, and sets the sticky
FDEBUG_TXOVER error flag for this FIFO.

fields.name: TXF1
fields.description:
Direct write access to the TX FIFO for this state machine. Each write
pushes one word to the FIFO. Attempting to write to a full FIFO has
no effect on the FIFO state or contents, and sets the sticky
FDEBUG_TXOVER error flag for this FIFO.

fields.name: TXF2
fields.description:
Direct write access to the TX FIFO for this state machine. Each write
pushes one word to the FIFO. Attempting to write to a full FIFO has
no effect on the FIFO state or contents, and sets the sticky
FDEBUG_TXOVER error flag for this FIFO.

fields.name: TXF3
fields.description:
Direct read access to the RX FIFO for this state machine. Each read
pops one word from the FIFO. Attempting to read from an empty FIFO
has no effect on the FIFO state, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_RXUNDER
error flag for this FIFO. The data returned to the system on a read
from an empty FIFO is undefined.

fields.name: RXF0
fields.description:
Direct read access to the RX FIFO for this state machine. Each read
pops one word from the FIFO. Attempting to read from an empty FIFO
has no effect on the FIFO state, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_RXUNDER
error flag for this FIFO. The data returned to the system on a read
from an empty FIFO is undefined.

fields.name: RXF1
fields.description:
Direct read access to the RX FIFO for this state machine. Each read
pops one word from the FIFO. Attempting to read from an empty FIFO
has no effect on the FIFO state, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_RXUNDER
error flag for this FIFO. The data returned to the system on a read
from an empty FIFO is undefined.

fields.name: RXF2
fields.description:
Direct read access to the RX FIFO for this state machine. Each read
pops one word from the FIFO. Attempting to read from an empty FIFO
has no effect on the FIFO state, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_RXUNDER
error flag for this FIFO. The data returned to the system on a read
from an empty FIFO is undefined.

fields.name: RXF3
fields.description:
State machine IRQ flags register. Write 1 to clear. There are 8 state
machine IRQ flags, which can be set, cleared, and waited on by the
state machines. There's no fixed association between flags and state
machines -- any state machine can use any flag.\n\n Any of the 8
flags can be used for timing synchronisation between state machines,
using IRQ and WAIT instructions. The lower four of these flags are
also routed out to system-level interrupt requests, alongside FIFO
status interrupts -- see e.g. IRQ0_INTE.

fields.name: IRQ
fields.description:
Writing a 1 to each of these bits will forcibly assert the
corresponding IRQ. Note this is different to the INTF register:
writing here affects PIO internal state. INTF just asserts the
processor-facing IRQ signal for testing ISRs, and is not visible to
the state machines.

fields.name: IRQ_FORCE
fields.description:
There is a 2-flipflop synchronizer on each GPIO input, which protects
PIO logic from metastabilities. This increases input delay, and for
fast synchronous IO (e.g. SPI) these synchronizers may need to be
bypassed. Each bit in this register corresponds to one GPIO.\n 0 ->
input is synchronized (default)\n 1 -> synchronizer is bypassed\n If
in doubt, leave this register as all zeroes.

fields.name: INPUT_SYNC_BYPASS
fields.description:
Read to sample the pad output values PIO is currently driving to the
GPIOs. On RP2040 there are 30 GPIOs, so the two most significant bits
are hardwired to 0.

fields.name: DBG_PADOUT
fields.description:
Read to sample the pad output enables (direction) PIO is currently
driving to the GPIOs. On RP2040 there are 30 GPIOs, so the two most
significant bits are hardwired to 0.

fields.name: DBG_PADOE
fields.description:
The PIO hardware has some free parameters that may vary between chip
products.\n These should be provided in the chip datasheet, but are
also exposed here.

fields.name: DBG_CFGINFO
fields.description: Write-only access to instruction memory location 0

fields.name: INSTR_MEM0
fields.description: Write-only access to instruction memory location 1

fields.name: INSTR_MEM1
fields.description: Write-only access to instruction memory location 2

fields.name: INSTR_MEM2
fields.description: Write-only access to instruction memory location 3

fields.name: INSTR_MEM3
fields.description: Write-only access to instruction memory location 4

fields.description: Write-only access to instruction memory location 5

fields.description: Write-only access to instruction memory location 22

fields.description: Write-only access to instruction memory location 23

fields.name: INSTR_MEM23
fields.description: Write-only access to instruction memory location 24

fields.name: INSTR_MEM24
fields.description: Write-only access to instruction memory location 25

fields.name: INSTR_MEM25
fields.description: Write-only access to instruction memory location 26

fields.name: INSTR_MEM26
fields.description: Write-only access to instruction memory location 27

fields.name: INSTR_MEM27
fields.description: Write-only access to instruction memory location 28

fields.name: INSTR_MEM28
fields.description: Write-only access to instruction memory location 29

fields.name: INSTR_MEM29
fields.description: Write-only access to instruction memory location 30

fields.name: INSTR_MEM30
fields.description: Write-only access to instruction memory location 31

fields.name: INSTR_MEM31
fields.description:
Clock divisor register for state machine 0\n Frequency = clock freq /
(CLKDIV_INT + CLKDIV_FRAC / 256)

fields.name: SM0_CLKDIV
fields.description: Execution/behavioural settings for state machine 0

fields.name: SM0_EXECCTRL
fields.description:
Control behaviour of the input/output shift registers for state
machine 0

fields.name: SM0_SHIFTCTRL
fields.description: Current instruction address of state machine 0

fields.name: SM0_ADDR
fields.description:
Read to see the instruction currently addressed by state machine 0's
program counter\n Write to execute an instruction immediately
(including jumps) and then resume execution.

fields.name: SM0_INSTR
fields.description: State machine pin control

fields.name: SM0_PINCTRL
fields.description:
Clock divisor register for state machine 1\n Frequency = clock freq /
(CLKDIV_INT + CLKDIV_FRAC / 256)

fields.name: SM1_CLKDIV
fields.description: Execution/behavioural settings for state machine 1

fields.name: SM1_EXECCTRL
fields.description:
Control behaviour of the input/output shift registers for state
machine 1

fields.name: SM1_SHIFTCTRL
fields.description: Current instruction address of state machine 1

fields.name: SM1_ADDR
fields.description:
Read to see the instruction currently addressed by state machine 1's
program counter\n Write to execute an instruction immediately
(including jumps) and then resume execution.

fields.name: SM1_INSTR
fields.description: State machine pin control

fields.name: SM1_PINCTRL
fields.description:
Clock divisor register for state machine 2\n Frequency = clock freq /
(CLKDIV_INT + CLKDIV_FRAC / 256)

fields.name: SM2_CLKDIV
fields.description: Execution/behavioural settings for state machine 2

fields.name: SM2_EXECCTRL
fields.description:
Control behaviour of the input/output shift registers for state
machine 2

fields.name: SM2_SHIFTCTRL
fields.description: Current instruction address of state machine 2

fields.name: SM2_ADDR
fields.description:
Read to see the instruction currently addressed by state machine 2's
program counter\n Write to execute an instruction immediately
(including jumps) and then resume execution.

fields.name: SM2_INSTR
fields.description: State machine pin control

fields.name: SM2_PINCTRL
fields.description:
Clock divisor register for state machine 3\n Frequency = clock freq /
(CLKDIV_INT + CLKDIV_FRAC / 256)

fields.name: SM3_CLKDIV
fields.description: Execution/behavioural settings for state machine 3

fields.name: SM3_EXECCTRL
fields.description:
Control behaviour of the input/output shift registers for state
machine 3

fields.name: SM3_SHIFTCTRL
fields.description: Current instruction address of state machine 3

fields.name: SM3_ADDR
fields.description:
Read to see the instruction currently addressed by state machine 3's
program counter\n Write to execute an instruction immediately
(including jumps) and then resume execution.

fields.name: SM3_INSTR
fields.description: State machine pin control

fields.name: SM3_PINCTRL
fields.description: Raw Interrupts

fields.name: INTR
fields.description: Interrupt Enable for irq0

fields.name: IRQ0_INTE
fields.description: Interrupt Force for irq0

fields.name: IRQ0_INTF
fields.description: Interrupt status after masking & forcing for irq0

fields.name: IRQ0_INTS
fields.description: Interrupt Enable for irq1

fields.name: IRQ1_INTE
fields.description: Interrupt Force for irq1

fields.name: IRQ1_INTF
fields.description: Interrupt status after masking & forcing for irq1

record_types.name: SM0_ADDR_Register

fields.name: SM0_ADDR
fields.description: unspecified

record_types.name: SM0_CLKDIV_Register

fields.name: Reserved_0_7
fields.description: Fractional part of clock divisor

fields.name: FRAC
fields.description:
Effective frequency is sysclk/(int + frac/256).\n Value of 0 is
interpreted as 65536. If INT is 0, FRAC must also be 0.

record_types.name: SM0_EXECCTRL_Register

fields.name: STATUS_N
fields.description: Comparison used for the MOV x, STATUS instruction.

fields.name: STATUS_SEL
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_5_6
fields.description: After reaching wrap_top, execution is wrapped to this address.

fields.name: WRAP_BOTTOM
fields.description:
After reaching this address, execution is wrapped to wrap_bottom.\n
If the instruction is a jump, and the jump condition is true, the
jump takes priority.

fields.name: WRAP_TOP
fields.description: Continuously assert the most recent OUT/SET to the pins

fields.name: OUT_STICKY
fields.description:
If 1, use a bit of OUT data as an auxiliary write enable\n When used
in conjunction with OUT_STICKY, writes with an enable of 0 will\n
deassert the latest pin write. This can create useful
masking/override behaviour\n due to the priority ordering of state
machine pin writes (SM0 < SM1 < ...)

fields.name: INLINE_OUT_EN
fields.description: Which data bit to use for inline OUT enable

fields.name: OUT_EN_SEL
fields.description:
The GPIO number to use as condition for JMP PIN. Unaffected by input
mapping.

fields.name: JMP_PIN
fields.description:
If 1, side-set data is asserted to pin directions, instead of pin
values

fields.name: SIDE_PINDIR
fields.description:
If 1, the MSB of the Delay/Side-set instruction field is used as
side-set enable, rather than a side-set data bit. This allows
instructions to perform side-set optionally, rather than on every
instruction, but the maximum possible side-set width is reduced from
5 to 4. Note that the value of PINCTRL_SIDESET_COUNT is inclusive of
this enable bit.

fields.name: SIDE_EN
fields.description:
Read-only. If 1, an instruction written to SMx_INSTR is stalled, and
latched by the state machine. Will clear to 0 once this instruction
completes.

record_types.name: SM0_INSTR_Register

fields.name: SM0_INSTR
fields.description: unspecified

record_types.name: SM0_PINCTRL_Register

fields.name: OUT_BASE
fields.description:
The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by a SET PINS or SET
PINDIRS instruction. The data written to this pin is the
least-significant bit of the SET data.

fields.name: SET_BASE
fields.description:
The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by a side-set
operation. The MSBs of an instruction's side-set/delay field (up to
5, determined by SIDESET_COUNT) are used for side-set data, with the
remaining LSBs used for delay. The least-significant bit of the
side-set portion is the bit written to this pin, with
more-significant bits written to higher-numbered pins.

fields.name: SIDESET_BASE
fields.description:
The pin which is mapped to the least-significant bit of a state
machine's IN data bus. Higher-numbered pins are mapped to
consecutively more-significant data bits, with a modulo of 32 applied
to pin number.

fields.name: IN_BASE
fields.description:
The number of pins asserted by an OUT PINS, OUT PINDIRS or MOV PINS
instruction. In the range 0 to 32 inclusive.

fields.name: OUT_COUNT
fields.description: The number of pins asserted by a SET. In the range 0 to 5 inclusive.

fields.name: SET_COUNT
fields.description:
The number of MSBs of the Delay/Side-set instruction field which are
used for side-set. Inclusive of the enable bit, if present. Minimum
of 0 (all delay bits, no side-set) and maximum of 5 (all side-set, no
delay).

record_types.name: SM0_SHIFTCTRL_Register

fields.name: Reserved_0_15
fields.description:
Push automatically when the input shift register is filled, i.e. on
an IN instruction which causes the input shift counter to reach or
exceed PUSH_THRESH.

fields.name: AUTOPUSH
fields.description:
Pull automatically when the output shift register is emptied, i.e. on
or following an OUT instruction which causes the output shift counter
to reach or exceed PULL_THRESH.

fields.name: AUTOPULL
fields.description:
1 = shift input shift register to right (data enters from left). 0 =
to left.

fields.name: IN_SHIFTDIR
fields.description: 1 = shift out of output shift register to right. 0 = to left.

fields.name: OUT_SHIFTDIR
fields.description:
Number of bits shifted into ISR before autopush, or conditional push
(PUSH IFFULL), will take place.\n Write 0 for value of 32.

fields.name: PUSH_THRESH
fields.description:
Number of bits shifted out of OSR before autopull, or conditional
pull (PULL IFEMPTY), will take place.\n Write 0 for value of 32.

fields.name: PULL_THRESH
fields.description:
When 1, TX FIFO steals the RX FIFO's storage, and becomes twice as
deep.\n RX FIFO is disabled as a result (always reads as both full
and empty).\n FIFOs are flushed when this bit is changed.

fields.name: FJOIN_TX
fields.description:
When 1, RX FIFO steals the TX FIFO's storage, and becomes twice as
deep.\n TX FIFO is disabled as a result (always reads as both full
and empty).\n FIFOs are flushed when this bit is changed.

record_types.name: SM1_ADDR_Register

fields.name: SM1_ADDR
fields.description: unspecified

record_types.name: SM1_CLKDIV_Register

fields.name: Reserved_0_7
fields.description: Fractional part of clock divisor

fields.name: FRAC
fields.description:
Effective frequency is sysclk/(int + frac/256).\n Value of 0 is
interpreted as 65536. If INT is 0, FRAC must also be 0.

record_types.name: SM1_EXECCTRL_Register

fields.name: STATUS_N
fields.description: Comparison used for the MOV x, STATUS instruction.

fields.name: STATUS_SEL
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_5_6
fields.description: After reaching wrap_top, execution is wrapped to this address.

fields.name: WRAP_BOTTOM
fields.description:
After reaching this address, execution is wrapped to wrap_bottom.\n
If the instruction is a jump, and the jump condition is true, the
jump takes priority.

fields.name: WRAP_TOP
fields.description: Continuously assert the most recent OUT/SET to the pins

fields.name: OUT_STICKY
fields.description:
If 1, use a bit of OUT data as an auxiliary write enable\n When used
in conjunction with OUT_STICKY, writes with an enable of 0 will\n
deassert the latest pin write. This can create useful
masking/override behaviour\n due to the priority ordering of state
machine pin writes (SM0 < SM1 < ...)

fields.name: INLINE_OUT_EN
fields.description: Which data bit to use for inline OUT enable

fields.name: OUT_EN_SEL
fields.description:
The GPIO number to use as condition for JMP PIN. Unaffected by input
mapping.

fields.name: JMP_PIN
fields.description:
If 1, side-set data is asserted to pin directions, instead of pin
values

fields.name: SIDE_PINDIR
fields.description:
If 1, the MSB of the Delay/Side-set instruction field is used as
side-set enable, rather than a side-set data bit. This allows
instructions to perform side-set optionally, rather than on every
instruction, but the maximum possible side-set width is reduced from
5 to 4. Note that the value of PINCTRL_SIDESET_COUNT is inclusive of
this enable bit.

fields.name: SIDE_EN
fields.description:
Read-only. If 1, an instruction written to SMx_INSTR is stalled, and
latched by the state machine. Will clear to 0 once this instruction
completes.

variables.name: PIO0_Periph
variables.code:
PIO0_Periph : aliased PIO_Peripheral
  with Import, Address => PIO0_Base;

variables.name: PIO1_Periph
variables.code:
PIO1_Periph : aliased PIO_Peripheral
  with Import, Address => PIO1_Base;
